primary_action.description: Register a complaint for power outage in your area with the local electricity distribution company.

escalation.authority: Electricity Ombudsman / State Electricity Regulatory Commission (SERC)
escalation.contact: Visit your state's SERC website for escalation procedures.
escalation.timeline: If unresolved within 30 days, escalate to the Electricity Ombudsman.

rti.how_to_file: File RTI under Section 6 of RTI Act, 2005 via https://rtionline.gov.in
rti.sample_query: Details of load shedding schedule, duration, and reasons for unscheduled outages in [your area] for the last 3 months.

primary_action.description: Register a complaint with LESA for power outage in Lucknow. You can also visit the nearest LESA office in your zone.

escalation.authority: Chief Engineer, LESA / UP Electricity Regulatory Commission (UPERC)
escalation.contact: Contact Chief Engineer LESA at 0522-2287747. Escalate to UPERC at www.uperc.org if unresolved.
escalation.timeline: LESA must respond within 48 hours. UPERC complaint within 30 days of non-resolution.

rti.how_to_file: File RTI under Section 6 of RTI Act, 2005 via https://rtionline.gov.in addressed to PIO, LESA Lucknow
rti.sample_query: Details of power outages reported in [your area/zone] of Lucknow, duration, and restoration time for the last 3 months.

primary_action.description: Register a complaint with JVVNL for power outage in Jaipur. Use the Rajasthan Sampark portal for online complaints.

escalation.authority: Superintending Engineer, JVVNL / Rajasthan Electricity Regulatory Commission (RERC)
escalation.contact: Contact SE of your JVVNL zone. Escalate to RERC at www.rerc.rajasthan.gov.in if unresolved.
escalation.timeline: JVVNL must acknowledge within 24 hours. RERC complaint after 15 days of non-resolution.

rti.how_to_file: File RTI under Section 6 of RTI Act, 2005 via https://rtionline.gov.in addressed to PIO, JVVNL Jaipur
rti.sample_query: Details of power outages in [your area] of Jaipur, including transformer failure reports and restoration timelines for the last 3 months.

primary_action.description: File a billing dispute with your DISCOM. Request a meter inspection and bill recalculation.

escalation.authority: Consumer Grievance Redressal Forum (CGRF) / Electricity Ombudsman
escalation.contact: Contact CGRF of your DISCOM. If unresolved, approach the Electricity Ombudsman.
escalation.timeline: CGRF must resolve within 45 days. Ombudsman appeal within 30 days of CGRF order.

rti.how_to_file: File RTI under Section 6 of RTI Act, 2005 via https://rtionline.gov.in
rti.sample_query: Provide tariff slab details, meter reading logs, and billing calculation methodology for consumer ID [your ID] for the past 6 months.

primary_action.department: Superintendent of Police (SP) / Commissioner of Police
primary_action.description: If the local police station refuses to file an FIR, approach the Superintendent of Police (SP) or Commissioner with a written complaint.

escalation.authority: Judicial Magistrate under Section 156(3) CrPC / State Human Rights Commission
escalation.contact: File an application before the Judicial Magistrate under Section 156(3) CrPC to direct FIR registration.
escalation.timeline: Magistrate can direct FIR registration immediately. SHRC complaint can be filed anytime.

rti.department: Office of the SP / Commissioner of Police
rti.how_to_file: File RTI under Section 6 of RTI Act, 2005 via https://rtionline.gov.in
rti.sample_query: Number of FIR registration refusals in [police station name] in the last 12 months and action taken on each.

primary_action.description: Approach the Commissioner of Police, Lucknow with a written complaint. You can also use the UP Police citizen portal or visit the nearest police station.

escalation.authority: IG Range Lucknow / Judicial Magistrate under Section 156(3) CrPC
escalation.contact: Contact IG Lucknow Range at 0522-2621509. File Section 156(3) application before the CJM Lucknow.
escalation.timeline: Commissioner must respond within 7 days. Magistrate can direct FIR registration immediately.

rti.department: Office of Commissioner of Police, Lucknow
rti.how_to_file: File RTI under Section 6 of RTI Act, 2005 via https://rtionline.gov.in to PIO, CP Office Lucknow
rti.sample_query: Number of FIR registration refusals at [police station name] in Lucknow in the last 12 months and action taken.

primary_action.helpline: Contact your local District Collector's office
primary_action.description: File a complaint with the Tehsildar or District Collector regarding land disputes. Request demarcation and verification of land records.

escalation.authority: Revenue Board / Civil Court / High Court
escalation.contact: Appeal to the Revenue Board or file a civil suit for title declaration. For encroachment on government land, contact the District Magistrate.
escalation.timeline: Revenue Board appeals within 60 days of Collector's order. Civil suits have no fixed timeline.

rti.how_to_file: File RTI under Section 6 of RTI Act, 2005 via https://rtionline.gov.in
rti.sample_query: Provide certified copies of land records (khasra, khatauni) for survey number [X] in village [Y], tehsil [Z], including mutation entries for the last 20 years.

primary_action.department: Public Works Department (PWD) / Municipal Corporation / NHAI
primary_action.description: Report road conditions to PWD for state roads, Municipal Corporation for city roads, or NHAI for national highways.

escalation.authority: District Magistrate / Chief Engineer PWD / NHAI Regional Office
escalation.contact: Escalate to the Chief Engineer (PWD) or NHAI Regional Officer. File a complaint on CPGRAMS (pgportal.gov.in).
escalation.timeline: CPGRAMS complaints are tracked with a 60-day resolution target.

rti.how_to_file: File RTI under Section 6 of RTI Act, 2005 via https://rtionline.gov.in
rti.sample_query: Provide details of road maintenance budget allocated and spent for [road name/number] in [district] for the current financial year, including contractor details.

primary_action.department: Jaipur Development Authority (JDA) / Jaipur Municipal Corporation (JMC)
primary_action.description: Report road conditions in Jaipur to JMC for city roads or JDA for development area roads. Use Rajasthan Sampark portal for online complaint tracking.

escalation.authority: Commissioner JMC / District Collector Jaipur / Chief Engineer PWD Rajasthan
escalation.contact: Escalate to Commissioner JMC at 0141-2227200 or District Collector Jaipur. File on CPGRAMS for central highways.
escalation.timeline: JMC must acknowledge within 3 days. Rajasthan Sampark tracks with a 30-day resolution target.

rti.how_to_file: File RTI under Section 6 of RTI Act, 2005 via https://rtionline.gov.in to PIO, JMC Jaipur
rti.sample_query: Road maintenance budget and expenditure for [road/area name] in Jaipur for the current financial year, including contractor and completion details.

primary_action.department: Public Health Engineering Department (PHED) / Municipal Water Supply Board
primary_action.helpline: Contact your local Jal Board / Municipal Corporation helpline
primary_action.description: Report water supply issues to the Public Health Engineering Department or your local Municipal Water Supply Board.

escalation.authority: District Magistrate / State Water Resources Department / National Green Tribunal (for contamination)
escalation.contact: Escalate to the District Magistrate or State Water Board. For contamination issues, file with the National Green Tribunal.
escalation.timeline: Municipal complaints: 15-day resolution target. NGT complaints: hearing within 30 days.

rti.how_to_file: File RTI under Section 6 of RTI Act, 2005 via https://rtionline.gov.in
rti.sample_query: Provide water quality test reports for [area/ward] for the last 6 months, including source details and treatment methods applied.

primary_action.department: Lucknow Jal Sansthan / Lucknow Municipal Corporation (LMC)
primary_action.description: Report water supply issues to Lucknow Jal Sansthan for piped water or LMC for tanker/emergency supply. Visit the nearest Jal Sansthan office for in-person complaint.

escalation.authority: General Manager, Jal Sansthan Lucknow / District Magistrate Lucknow
escalation.contact: Contact GM Jal Sansthan at 0522-2205785. Escalate to DM Lucknow for urgent issues.
escalation.timeline: Jal Sansthan must respond within 24 hours for supply disruption. DM intervention for contamination.

rti.how_to_file: File RTI under Section 6 of RTI Act, 2005 via https://rtionline.gov.in to PIO, Jal Sansthan Lucknow
rti.sample_query: Water quality test reports for [ward/mohalla] in Lucknow for the last 6 months, chlorination records, and source details.

primary_action.description: File a complaint with the Regional Transport Office against overcharging. Note the vehicle registration number and details of the incident.

escalation.contact: Escalate to the Transport Commissioner. For financial loss, file a complaint with the District Consumer Forum.
escalation.timeline: RTO action within 15 days. Consumer Forum: hearing within 21 days of filing.

rti.how_to_file: File RTI under Section 6 of RTI Act, 2005 via https://rtionline.gov.in
rti.sample_query: Provide the authorized fare chart for auto-rickshaws/taxis in [city], number of overcharging complaints received and resolved in the last 12 months.

primary_action.department: Cyber Crime Cell / National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal
primary_action.description: Report cybercrime on the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal immediately. Call 1930 for financial fraud to freeze transactions.

escalation.authority: State Cyber Crime Cell / CERT-In / Judiciary
escalation.contact: Escalate to the State Cyber Crime Cell SP. For large-scale fraud, report to CERT-In (cert-in.org.in).
escalation.timeline: Financial fraud helpline (1930): Immediate action. Portal complaints: Tracked with case ID.

rti.department: Cyber Crime Cell / Ministry of Home Affairs
rti.how_to_file: File RTI under Section 6 of RTI Act, 2005 via https://rtionline.gov.in
rti.sample_query: Number of cybercrime complaints received and resolved through cybercrime.gov.in in [state] for the last financial year, categorized by type of crime.

primary_action.department: Central Ground Water Authority (CGWA) / State Ground Water Department
primary_action.helpline: Contact your State Ground Water Authority
primary_action.description: Report illegal borewells to the Central Ground Water Authority or the State Ground Water Department. Unauthorized extraction is punishable under the Environment Protection Act.

escalation.contact: File a complaint before the National Green Tribunal. Also inform the District Magistrate for local enforcement action.
escalation.timeline: NGT: hearing within 30 days. CGWA: action within 60 days.

rti.how_to_file: File RTI under Section 6 of RTI Act, 2005 via https://rtionline.gov.in
rti.sample_query: Number of authorized and unauthorized borewells in [district/block], action taken against illegal borewells in the last 2 years.

primary_action.description: Apply for mutation at the Tehsildar's office with sale deed, death certificate (if inheritance), and identity proof. Follow up on pending applications.

escalation.authority: Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM) / Additional Collector / Revenue Board
escalation.contact: Appeal to the SDM or Additional Collector if mutation is delayed or rejected. Further appeal to the Revenue Board.
escalation.timeline: Mutation should be completed within 30-45 days. Appeal to SDM within 30 days of rejection.

rti.how_to_file: File RTI under Section 6 of RTI Act, 2005 via https://rtionline.gov.in
rti.sample_query: Status of mutation application number [X] filed on [date], total pending mutation cases in [tehsil], and average processing time.
